title: agropyron intermedium subsp. trichophorum
intro: Agropyron intermedium subsp. trichophorum, also known as meadow barley, is a type of grass that belongs to the genus Agropyron and is native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of meadow barley include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Meadow barley can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
